name: Drawbridge over a moat
description: Straight wooden span bridging a moat, drawn in a partially-lifted pose. A flat deck of @primary planks runs along the longer horizontal axis at y0; two @accent anchor posts (2 blocks tall, capped with minecraft:lantern) stand at the inner (building-side) end on either side; two slanted chains of minecraft:iron_bars rise from the outer end up to the top of the anchor posts, suggesting the bridge is being winched up. Optional decorative bumps every 2 blocks along the outer deck edges are @stairs facing outward — planks lifted in a rhythm. Length follows max(W, D); width is clamped to 2-3.

required_furniture.rule: Fill of @primary at y0 covering the full deck rectangle; spans max(W, D).
Two minecraft:iron_bars chains, one on each long side, slanting UP from outer end (y0+1) to inner anchor-post top (y0+2).
Two 2-tall @accent posts at the inner (building-side) end, one on each side of the width axis, starting at y0+1.
minecraft:lantern on top of each anchor post (y0+3); allowed to protrude one block above the AABB envelope.
Every 2 blocks along the outer deck edges, an @stairs block facing outward — only when span_len >= 6 and width >= 3.

placement_rules: Span runs along whichever horizontal axis is longer (max(W, D))
Chains slant up to suggest the drawbridge being lifted
Lanterns hang on top of the anchor posts (may protrude 1 above AABB)
lighting_ventilation: Two lanterns atop the anchor posts illuminate the bridge mouth; the deck itself is open-air, so ventilation is implicit. Chains and the iron-bar railing read as defensive, not enclosed.

medieval.notes: Dark-oak planks with iron_bars chains over a stone anchorage; classic castle-mouth drawbridge.

modern.notes: Smooth-stone deck with iron_bars chains — reads as a service bridge rather than a defensive one.

fantasy.notes: Dark-oak deck with proper minecraft:chain links instead of iron_bars; purpur anchor posts for arcane reading.

python_skill_signature: build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: Medieval / fortified-military motif. Coherent in medieval/castle/rustic/fantasy/gothic. Out of place in Modern, Minimalist, East-Asian, Classical/Renaissance buildings.
